detail: Supported (faithful implementation, not a measurement): the drive is a software integrator that self-drains at exactly 1.00x nominal (degenerate CI, as expected for a software integrator) and samples every 2.3s across 12 runs / 46 h, autonomously — incl. 2 runs with no visitors.

Supported (faithful implementation, not a measurement): labels are derived from level by the coded 60/25 thresholds, so transitions bracket them by implementation (acc 1.00/1.00); the non-trivial result is near-zero flapping (0 reversals) around the boundaries.

Supported: deficit state is associated with a state-contingent shift in robot action selection. Face-to-face hunger framing jumps 3% -> 66% (x24); feed-seeking speech acts go 1 -> 20 (deficit-only); co-present feeding pursuit 0.15 -> 0.43 with larger meals (21 -> 31); and the remote channel fires 172 proactive pings in deficit vs 0 at Full. Feeding-pursuit OR 4.9 [2.6,9.5] (person-clustered GEE; bootstrap 4.4 [2.1,8.0]). This is the deficit-to-action-selection evidence: the deficit biases goal-directed recovery behaviour in both channels. (Pings/feed-seeking are coded gates = faithful implementation; framing/pursuit/meal-size are emergent measurements of how strongly the deficit reshapes behaviour.)

Supported: when Starving, social-completion behaviour is strongly suppressed (turns diff -2.39, Engaged 0.08 vs 0.68; OR 0.03 [0.01,0.14], person-clustered GEE; bootstrap 0.03 [0.00,0.08]) while feeding pursuit rises (0.54 vs 0.26) — priority reallocation toward recovery, not undifferentiated disengagement. Starving n=13 (small-n; directional).

Supported: meal size rises with deficit {'Full': 21.0, 'Hungry': 29.0, 'Starving': 43.0}; proactive Telegram pings drew replies at 0.21 [0.15,0.26]; recovery is drive-initiated (proactive) not merely reactive.

claim: Observed Starving episodes resolve by feeding
detail: Supported (exploratory): in the observed Starving episodes, 100% received a feed, 100% escaped Starving via feeding, and 100% recovered to Full via feeding (n=8). This is an operational status check, not a population recovery rate or a guarantee. Overall reliability (RQ2-c) is carried by the low long-run starvation occupancy (B7), not by these 8 episodes nor by the modest 21% remote ping-response rate: the robot seldom reached Starving because human engagement maintained the regulatory loop.

claim: Replenishment is reliable at the loop level (always-on, pooled long-run)
detail: Supported (supporting reliability observation): modelled long-run Starving occupancy is median 1.0% [95% 0.2, 3.1%] (run-level block bootstrap; transition-level Poisson bootstrap 1.1% [0.4, 2.3%] agrees) — i.e. human feeding responses maintained the robot outside Starving for ~98-100% of the time; no absorbing state. This is not a controller-only property: the transition rates record the coupled human-robot regulatory loop, so the interpretation is that human engagement, with demonstrated participation from the drive (see B5), reliably replenished the robot. The point estimate 1.1% is fragile, so we lead with the interval; single condition means the drive's exact causal share in the feeding is not isolated. STATIONARITY CAVEAT: the CTMC is time-homogeneous, but the deployment is not — it pools visited daytime runs, idle no-visitor runs, and Phase 1/2 into one generator, so the 'long-run occupancy' is the stationary fraction of a pooled process, not of any single real operating regime. The pooling is conservative here (idle runs contribute drain/down-transitions with no feeding counterpart, pushing Starving UP), so the true visited-regime occupancy is if anything lower; read the interval as an order-of-magnitude ceiling, not a calibrated rate.

claim: Adaptive regulatory memory tracks interaction history and is expressed downstream
detail: Supported: the adaptive regulatory memory tracks socially embedded interaction history and is subsequently expressed in proactive allocation. Manipulation check — obligated feeders fed 2.7x the unconstrained rate in Phase 1 [1.2,5.9] (bootstrap 2.7 [1.0,8.4]) and the no-feed pair complied perfectly (0 feeds in Phase 1), with the feeder excess shrinking 0.43x once roles lifted. Core model — +1 SD interaction duration predicts affinity change +0.17 [+0.12,+0.21] (bootstrap +0.17 [+0.06,+0.30]) for unconstrained people, moderated by role (for obligated feeders, reward delivery rather than chat duration dominated the update) and attenuated in Phase 2; all three dose definitions agree in sign; LOPO-stable. Downstream policy expression — prior affinity increases next-day proactive approaches 1.55x per +1 affinity [1.09,2.20] (bootstrap 1.65 [1.19,2.75]) beyond activity. Caveat: 2 people per controlled role (validation, not population inference). Repair-robustness — excluding the 2 canonicalization-merged identities (whose affinity was partly reconstructed) leaves the duration slope at +0.23 [+0.17,+0.29] (p=7.4e-14), so the association is not induced by the EMA re-threading.
